description: Stage 16 pilot for relational feedback dynamics on the frozen projected transverse architecture.
selection_rationale: The pilot uses one representative that already showed stable phase-ordering, one transport corridor with stable local ordering, and one clustered composite anchor that remained weak but structured in Stage 15. Each case is paired with a single relational driver so the first probe stays hypothesis-driven rather than atlas-like.

base_seed_reference.selection_note: strongest coherent periodic transverse seed carried through Stages 11-16

resolution_policy.followup_gate: Only extend a case to the refinement ladder if the base-resolution pilot shows measurable stabilization over null without global artificial binding.
resolution_policy.selection_note: Reuses the Stage 15 ladder so ordering and dynamic response stay directly comparable.

relational_feedback.bounded_normalization: row-normalized relational weights with max row sum 1
relational_feedback.graph_locality: weights built only from the selected Stage 15 relational driver for that case
relational_feedback.scale_coupling: same relational construction at N and 2N with no change to kernel, projector, or base operator

cases.selection_note: best non-degenerate Stage 15 ordering carrier; used to test selective stabilization in a phase-ordered regime
transport-dominated control; tests whether the only corridor with stable delay ordering develops selective feedback response

cases.driver_strength_class: weak ordering with high metric violation
cases.selection_note: composite anchor stays in the pilot because Stage 16 should test whether weak relational order can begin to feed back into the strongest composite morphology

positive_only_if: stabilization appears only in cases that already showed metric-like ordering
decision_gate.otherwise: classify as diagnostic negative and conclude relational ordering has not yet condensed into effective dynamics
